title: Location geocoding workflow
text: clean_addresses() adds *_clean columns full_address_clean string; flag_bad_addresses() sends PO boxes placeholders straight review. address city required. file ID, locatr generates row-number IDs; ZIP, zip_clean stays NA single-line address omits ZIP instead ending NA: Missing ZIP recorded bad_address_flag == "missing_zip" audit, block geocoding address + city + state present.
code: library(locatr)  cleaned <- records %>%   clean_addresses(     id = `Location ID`, address = Address,     city = City, zip = Zip, name = `Location Name`   ) %>%   flag_bad_addresses() cleaned_minimal <- records %>%   clean_addresses(address = Address, city = City, state = "NJ") %>%   flag_bad_addresses()

title: Location geocoding workflow
text: geocode_records() runs tier turn validates configured region pass, later, fuzzier service retries still unplaced. geocode_pass column records tier placed row.
code: geocoded <- geocode_records(cleaned)   # Census -> ArcGIS -> name lookup

title: Location geocoding workflow
text: geography step independent input address columns coordinates exist. add_county_muni() builds Census TIGER/Line geography attaches county/locality fields. Pass sf boundary layer adapt geography join, e.g. add_muni_from_shapes(geocoded, muni_shapes = my_local_shapes), use add_muni_from_key() records geography share code column.
code: with_geography <- add_county_muni(geocoded, state = "NJ")

title: Location geocoding workflow
text: final ready Tableau, GIS joins, reusable reference table, every row carries audit columns explain coordinate produced.
code: write_geocode_review(with_geography, "manual_review.csv")  final <- with_geography %>%   apply_manual_overrides("manual_review_completed.csv") %>%   export_location_crosswalk("location_crosswalk.csv")

title: Authors and Citation
text: George Arthur. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Arthur G (2026). locatr: Audit-Ready Geocoding Local Geography Messy Location Data. R package version 0.1.0, https://prigasg.github.io/locatr/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {locatr: Audit-Ready Geocoding and Local Geography for Messy Location Data},   author = {George Arthur},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://prigasg.github.io/locatr/}, }

title: locatr
text: audit-ready R toolkit cleaning, geocoding, validating, reviewing, exporting messy address location data. sits top tidygeocoder: tidygeocoder fetches coordinates; locatr decides whether coordinates trustworthy produces dashboard- GIS-ready crosswalk.

title: locatr
text: Geocoders imperfect. Strict services miss real addresses; fuzzy services can confidently place bad match far outside intended service area. locatr wraps reality three guards: Flagging - PO boxes, placeholders, missing address/city fields never hit geocoder. ZIP helpful optional; missing ZIP audited without blocking address + city + state geocoding. Fallback passes - ArcGIS name-based lookup can pick stricter geocoder misses. Region validation - anything lands outside configured bounding box boundary polygon rejected, mapped. Every function records audit trail (geocode_method, geocode_pass, match_status, validation_status, review_status, manual_override_used) reviewer can see coordinate produced. finished geocoding output, review_status uses outcome-oriented values auto_accepted, needs_manual_review, manual_override_applied, rejected.

title: locatr
code: # from a local clone devtools::document() devtools::install()

title: locatr
text: minimum, clean_addresses() needs address city. ID supplied, locatr creates row-number IDs; ZIP supplied, zip_clean stays NA single-line address omits trailing ZIP: Rows missing ZIP remain geocodable. Rows missing address/city, PO boxes, placeholder addresses, test records still go manual review.
code: library(locatr)  cleaned <- records %>%   clean_addresses(id = `Facility ID`, address = Address,                   city = City, zip = Zip, name = `Facility Name`,                   state = "NJ") %>%   flag_bad_addresses()  geocoded <- geocode_records(cleaned, bbox = region_bbox("NJ"))  with_geography <- add_county_muni(geocoded, state = "NJ")  write_geocode_review(with_geography, "manual_review.csv")  final <- with_geography %>%   apply_manual_overrides("manual_review_completed.csv", bbox = region_bbox("NJ")) %>%   export_location_crosswalk("location_crosswalk.csv") cleaned <- records %>%   clean_addresses(address = Address, city = City, state = "NJ") %>%   flag_bad_addresses()

title: locatr
text: users rather write R, pipeline available Shiny app: upload CSV/Excel/Parquet file, geocode , download geocoded records immediately CSV, Excel, Parquet. also need county/locality fields, attach geography Census TIGER/Line uploaded shapefile first, download geography crosswalk. download step lets remove columns exporting. app, Unique ID ZIP optional: leave (auto) / (none) file address city. app also published Hugging Face Space (Docker): https://huggingface.co/spaces/Prigas89/locatr_reviewer. deployment scaffolding lives huggingface/.
code: install.packages(c("shiny", "bslib", "DT", "leaflet",                    "readxl", "writexl", "arrow")) run_locatr_app()

title: locatr
text: geocode_records() runs progressively fuzzier internet services, retrying rows began geocodable addresses still unplaced validation pass: Tier 0 optional authoritative: pass geocode_records(cleaned, reference = verified) call backfill_from_reference() directly place records whose coordinates checked . rows bbox-validated, stamped pass_0_reference, skipped every later tier. input readiness value ready_for_geocoding internal cascade; geocode_records() completes, valid matched rows marked auto_accepted. Name lookup score-gated geocoder returns score/type fields. Precise point-address hits min_score pass cleanly; fuzzier POI, locality, low-score hits keep coordinates reviewer context remain needs_manual_review. Shiny app exposes name score threshold address types allowed pass without review.

title: locatr
text: geocoding, validation, ArcGIS search extent, pass bbox: stricter validation, pass sf boundary polygon boundary =.
code: custom_bbox <- c(lat_min = 38.0, lat_max = 39.0, lon_min = -77.5, lon_max = -76.0) geocoded <- geocode_records(cleaned, bbox = custom_bbox)

title: locatr
text: production, location_locality location_county come spatial join authoritative NJ municipal boundary layer, geocoder response Census reverse-geocoding. workflow : load boundaries, derive bbox, geocode, validate, convert sf points, spatially join polygons, write location_county, location_locality, geography_match_status. build packaged default data-raw/local_geography.R, sourced NJGIN/NJOGIS Municipal Boundaries NJ.
code: munis <- sf::st_read("path/to/Municipal_Boundaries_of_NJ.shp") %>%   sf::st_make_valid() %>%   dplyr::transmute(location_county = COUNTY, location_locality = MUN)  bbox <- bbox_from_sf(munis) geocoded <- geocode_records(cleaned, bbox = bbox)  with_geography <- add_local_geography(   geocoded,   geography_shapes = munis,   county_col   = "location_county",   locality_col = "location_locality" )

title: locatr
text: add_county_muni() shortest path Census TIGER/Line boundaries good enough workflow: hood, build_local_geography() pulls Census boundaries tigris standardises location_county / location_locality schema, plus stable join fields source provides : county_code, county_fips, municipality_code, municipality_geoid, municipality_name_standard, municipality_type, muni_join_key. Pick geography state: county easy everywhere; county_subdivision maps well townships/municipalities NJ, PA, NY, New England; place covers incorporated places/CDPs misses townships many unincorporated areas; tract uses tract GEOIDs. high-stakes, state-specific reporting “municipality” legal meaning, use official state GIS layer pass directly. Muni Key kept readable fallback, production joins prefer muni_join_key, municipality_geoid, another official code boundary source. Use add_muni_from_shapes() point--polygon joins boundary layer, add_muni_from_key() records geography share code column ZIP, FIPS, GEOID.
code: final <- add_county_muni(geocoded, state = "PA", geography = "county_subdivision") areas <- build_local_geography(state = "PA", geography = "county_subdivision") bbox <- bbox_from_sf(areas)  geocoded <- geocode_records(cleaned, bbox = bbox)  final <- add_local_geography(   geocoded,   geography_shapes = areas,   county_col   = "location_county",   locality_col = "location_locality" )

what: Add county and municipality fields by joining on a shared key — add_muni_from_key
title: Add county and municipality fields by joining on a shared key — add_muni_from_key
text: non-spatial counterpart add_muni_from_shapes(). Instead point--polygon join, merges geography attributes boundary layer onto geocoded records shared key column (example ZIP, FIPS, GEOID, local region code tables carry). Use spatial join right criterion - records may lack coordinates, authoritative geography keyed code rather polygon footprint.

title: Add county and municipality fields by joining on a shared key — add_muni_from_key
text: data data frame geocoded records carrying data_key. muni_shapes sf polygon (attribute) layer carrying shp_key geography attributes attach. data_key Name (string) join-key column data. shp_key Name (string) join-key column muni_shapes. county_col, muni_col Optional explicit county / locality column names muni_shapes. Empty strings treated unset. key_col Optional municipal key column muni_shapes.

title: Add county and municipality fields by joining on a shared key — add_muni_from_key
text: data County, Municipality, Muni Key, muni_join_key, county_code, county_fips, municipality_code, municipality_geoid, municipality_name_standard, municipality_type, muni_match_status, generic location_county / location_locality / geography_match_status audit fields.

title: Add county and municipality fields by joining on a shared key — add_muni_from_key
text: Output columns match add_muni_from_shapes() exactly, two join paths interchangeable downstream: County, Municipality, Muni Key, muni_join_key, stable code/identifier fields, location_county, location_locality, geography_match_status, muni_match_status. Stable code fields (county_code, municipality_code, municipality_geoid, etc.) auto-detected common boundary schemas; county_fips synthesised state FIPS plus county code supplied directly, Muni Key falls back County::Municipality official identifier exists.

title: Apply completed manual overrides — apply_manual_overrides

title: Apply completed manual overrides — apply_manual_overrides
code: apply_manual_overrides(data, override_file, bbox = region_bbox("NJ"))

title: Apply completed manual overrides — apply_manual_overrides
text: data data frame record_id audit columns. override_file Path completed override CSV. bbox Bounding box validating manual coordinates; see region_bbox().

title: Apply completed manual overrides — apply_manual_overrides
text: data overrides applied manual_override_used set.

what: Backfill verified coordinates from a trusted reference table (Tier 0) — backfill_from_reference
title: Backfill verified coordinates from a trusted reference table (Tier 0) — backfill_from_reference
text: authoritative first tier cascade. Joins coordinates curated key -> coordinates table - institutional-memory table records whose location resolved checked point - automated geocoders, record already verified never re-geocoded. turns one analyst's manual review permanent asset: feed last cycle's completed overrides (trusted coordinate list) back reference, rows placed instantly exactly.

title: Backfill verified coordinates from a trusted reference table (Tier 0) — backfill_from_reference
code: backfill_from_reference(   data,   reference,   by = "record_id",   lat_col = "latitude",   lon_col = "longitude",   county_col = NULL,   locality_col = NULL,   bbox = region_bbox("NJ") )

title: Backfill verified coordinates from a trusted reference table (Tier 0) — backfill_from_reference
text: data data frame flag_bad_addresses() (frame carrying key column). reference data frame verified records: key column plus coordinate columns. May also carry county/locality columns. NULL empty frame makes -op. Name key column shared data reference (default "record_id"). lat_col, lon_col Coordinate column names reference (default "latitude"/"longitude"). county_col, locality_col Optional geography column names reference backfill location_county/location_locality. bbox Bounding box used reject --region reference coordinates; see region_bbox().

title: Backfill verified coordinates from a trusted reference table (Tier 0) — backfill_from_reference
text: data reference audit columns ref_latitude, ref_longitude, ref_status, , rows reference filled, updated latitude/longitude/geocode_method/geocode_pass ("pass_0_reference")/ match_status/review_status.

title: Backfill verified coordinates from a trusted reference table (Tier 0) — backfill_from_reference
text: Reference coordinates still bbox-validated, stale fat-fingered entry drop point outside region. reference authoritative, matched row marked review_status == "reference_backfilled" carries valid coordinates, geocode_census() every later tier skip automatically - even raw address previously flagged (e.g. PO box whose true coordinates verified ). Run geocode_census() (cascade pass reference = geocode_records()).

title: Backfill verified coordinates from a trusted reference table (Tier 0) — backfill_from_reference
code: records <- tibble::tibble(   record_id = c("a", "b"),   review_status = c("ready_for_geocoding", "needs_manual_review") ) verified <- tibble::tibble(record_id = "b", latitude = 40.22, longitude = -74.76) backfill_from_reference(records, verified) #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>   record_id review_status        latitude longitude geocode_method geocode_pass  #>   <chr>     <chr>                   <dbl>     <dbl> <chr>          <chr>         #> 1 a         ready_for_geocoding      NA        NA   NA             NA            #> 2 b         reference_backfilled     40.2     -74.8 reference      pass_0_refer… #> # ℹ 4 more variables: match_status <chr>, ref_latitude <dbl>, #> #   ref_longitude <dbl>, ref_status <chr>

what: Build a geocoding bounding box from an sf layer — bbox_from_sf
title: Build a geocoding bounding box from an sf layer — bbox_from_sf
text: Converts point, line, polygon sf layer WGS84 returns named latitude/longitude bounding box suitable geocode_records(), geocode_arcgis(), geocode_by_name(), validate_geocodes(). safest way keep multi-state geocoding local geography joins aligned: build load geography layer first, derive bbox .

title: Build a geocoding bounding box from an sf layer — bbox_from_sf
code: bbox_from_sf(geography_shapes, buffer = 0.05)

title: Build a geocoding bounding box from an sf layer — bbox_from_sf
text: geography_shapes sf object. buffer Numeric buffer decimal degrees added side bounding box. Defaults 0.05 avoid rejecting edge locations.

title: Build a geocoding bounding box from an sf layer — bbox_from_sf
text: named numeric vector lat_min, lat_max, lon_min, lon_max.

title: Build a geocoding bounding box from an sf layer — bbox_from_sf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ areas <- build_local_geography("PA") bbox <- bbox_from_sf(areas) } # }

what: Build a local geography layer from Census TIGER/Line boundaries — build_local_geography
title: Build a local geography layer from Census TIGER/Line boundaries — build_local_geography
text: Downloads authoritative Census boundary layer state tigris package standardises two-column schema add_local_geography() expects: location_county (always counties) location_locality (requested geography). also carries stable Census identifiers county_fips, municipality_geoid, muni_join_key fields exist. makes "locality" configurable concept, counts municipality consistent across states.

title: Build a local geography layer from Census TIGER/Line boundaries — build_local_geography
code: build_local_geography(   state,   geography = c("county_subdivision", "place", "county", "tract"),   county = NULL,   year = NULL,   cb = TRUE,   ... )

title: Build a local geography layer from Census TIGER/Line boundaries — build_local_geography
text: state Two-letter state abbreviation FIPS code (passed tigris). geography Census layer becomes location_locality. One "county_subdivision", "place", "county", "tract". county Optional county filter (name FIPS) "county_subdivision"/"tract"; passed tigris. year Vintage year boundary files. NULL uses tigris default. cb TRUE (default), use smaller cartographic boundary files; FALSE pulls full-resolution TIGER/Line files. ... Passed underlying tigris download function.

title: Build a local geography layer from Census TIGER/Line boundaries — build_local_geography
text: sf polygon layer WGS84 (EPSG:4326) location_county, location_locality, stable join-code columns available, geometry, ready add_local_geography(geography_shapes = ...).

title: Build a local geography layer from Census TIGER/Line boundaries — build_local_geography
text: Choosing geography: "county_subdivision" (default) maps well townships/municipalities states like NJ, PA, NY New England. Best general default "locality". "place" maps incorporated places CDPs, misses townships many unincorporated areas. Places can also straddle counties, place assigned county overlaps . "county" sets locality county . "tract" uses census tract identifier locality (analysis, administrative naming). Census TIGER/Line best scalable baseline nationwide. high-stakes, state-specific reporting "municipality" legal meaning, prefer official state GIS layer pass straight add_local_geography() - path works regardless, since join accepts polygon layer.

title: Build a local geography layer from Census TIGER/Line boundaries — build_local_geography
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ areas <- build_local_geography(state = "PA", geography = "county_subdivision") final <- add_local_geography(geocoded, geography_shapes = areas) } # }

what: Clean and standardise address fields — clean_addresses
title: Clean and standardise address fields — clean_addresses
text: Normalises raw address, city ZIP text geocoder-friendly columns builds single-line full_address_clean. Column mappings supplied tidy-eval (bare column names). Original address pieces preserved *_raw columns. input already contains full_address_clean column case style (example Full_Address_Clean), locatr preserves user-supplied value full_address_raw one canonical full_address_clean column cleaning.

title: Clean and standardise address fields — clean_addresses
code: clean_addresses(   data,   id = NULL,   address,   city,   zip = NULL,   name = NULL,   state = "NJ" )

title: Clean and standardise address fields — clean_addresses
text: data data frame records addresses. id Optional bare column name holding unique record identifier. omitted, record_id generated row number. address, city Bare column names raw address city. Required. zip Optional bare column name raw ZIP/postal code. omitted, zip_clean NA. name Optional bare column name record name (kept record_name review/exports). Defaults NULL. state Two-letter state used rows. Defaults "NJ" first production workflow; pass another state abbreviation needed.

title: Clean and standardise address fields — clean_addresses
text: data added columns: record_id, record_name, address_raw, city_raw, zip_raw, optional full_address_raw, address_clean, city_clean, state_clean, zip_clean, full_address_clean.

title: Clean and standardise address fields — clean_addresses
text: address city required. id omitted, surrogate record_id generated row position. zip omitted (empty), zip_clean NA full_address_clean built without trailing ZIP, address + city + state row still geocodable. Supplying ZIP improves Census structured-match precision longer mandatory.

title: Clean and standardise address fields — clean_addresses
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   LocationID = "NJ306100", Name = "Hackensack-UMC Mountainside",   Address = "ONE BAY AVE", City = "Montclair", Zip = "7042" ) clean_addresses(df, id = LocationID, address = Address,                        city = City, zip = Zip, name = Name) #> # A tibble: 1 × 15 #>   LocationID Name     Address City  Zip   record_id address_raw city_raw zip_raw #>   <chr>      <chr>    <chr>   <chr> <chr> <chr>     <chr>       <chr>    <chr>   #> 1 NJ306100   Hackens… ONE BA… Mont… 7042  NJ306100  ONE BAY AVE Montcla… 7042    #> # ℹ 6 more variables: state_clean <chr>, address_clean <chr>, city_clean <chr>, #> #   zip_clean <chr>, full_address_clean <chr>, record_name <chr>  # address + city only (surrogate id, no ZIP) clean_addresses(tibble::tibble(Address = "100 Main St", City = "Trenton"),                 address = Address, city = City) #> # A tibble: 1 × 12 #>   Address City  record_id address_raw city_raw zip_raw state_clean address_clean #>   <chr>   <chr> <chr>     <chr>       <chr>    <chr>   <chr>       <chr>         #> 1 100 Ma… Tren… 1         100 Main St Trenton  NA      NJ          100 MAIN STR… #> # ℹ 4 more variables: city_clean <chr>, zip_clean <chr>, #> #   full_address_clean <chr>, record_name <chr>

title: Export the location crosswalk — export_location_crosswalk
text: Selects final, stable set columns dashboards, GIS joins, reusable reference tables, optionally writes CSV. Audit columns retained reviewer can always see coordinate produced, including score/type/status fields name lookup tier available.

title: Export the location crosswalk — export_location_crosswalk
code: export_location_crosswalk(data, path = NULL)

title: Export the location crosswalk — export_location_crosswalk
text: data fully processed data frame. path Optional output CSV path. NULL, nothing written.

title: Export the location crosswalk — export_location_crosswalk
text: crosswalk tibble (also written path supplied).

what: Flag addresses that should not be blindly geocoded — flag_bad_addresses
title: Flag addresses that should not be blindly geocoded — flag_bad_addresses
text: Identifies PO boxes, placeholders, missing fields go straight review instead wasting geocoder calls (producing confident--wrong matches). Sets bad_address_flag initial review_status.

title: Flag addresses that should not be blindly geocoded — flag_bad_addresses
code: flag_bad_addresses(data)

title: Flag addresses that should not be blindly geocoded — flag_bad_addresses
text: data data frame clean_addresses().

title: Flag addresses that should not be blindly geocoded — flag_bad_addresses
text: data added columns bad_address_flag review_status. Rows fit geocoding get review_status == "ready_for_geocoding".

title: Flag addresses that should not be blindly geocoded — flag_bad_addresses
text: missing ZIP recorded bad_address_flag == "missing_zip" audit, block geocoding: long address city present, row stays ready_for_geocoding (Census matches street/city/state ArcGIS single-line address). genuinely unusable rows - missing address city, PO boxes, placeholders, test records - routed needs_manual_review.

title: Flag addresses that should not be blindly geocoded — flag_bad_addresses
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   record_id = c("a", "b"),   address_clean = c("100 MAIN STREET", "PO BOX 42"),   city_clean = c("TRENTON", "TRENTON"),   zip_clean = c("08608", "08608"),   record_name = c("Real Site", "Mailbox Co") ) flag_bad_addresses(df) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   record_id address_clean   city_clean zip_clean record_name bad_address_flag #>   <chr>     <chr>           <chr>      <chr>     <chr>       <chr>            #> 1 a         100 MAIN STREET TRENTON    08608     Real Site   NA               #> 2 b         PO BOX 42       TRENTON    08608     Mailbox Co  po_box           #> # ℹ 1 more variable: review_status <chr>

what: ArcGIS address fallback pass (Google-like fuzzy matching) — geocode_arcgis
title: ArcGIS address fallback pass (Google-like fuzzy matching) — geocode_arcgis
text: rows Census pass place inside configured region, re-geocodes composite geocoder (ArcGIS default: free, API key, fuzzy matching close Google) using single-line full_address_clean. ArcGIS requests constrained region bbox possible, results still guarded bounding box --region false matches discarded coordinates coalesced back latitude/longitude.

title: ArcGIS address fallback pass (Google-like fuzzy matching) — geocode_arcgis
code: geocode_arcgis(data, method = "arcgis", bbox = region_bbox("NJ"), ...)

title: ArcGIS address fallback pass (Google-like fuzzy matching) — geocode_arcgis

title: Name-based geocode pass (the
text: rows still unplaced address-based passes, geocodes record name plus city state rather street line. can resolve campus/landmark addresses (e.g. unit inside hospital) street-range interpolation , composite geocoder recognises named place.

title: Name-based geocode pass (the
code: geocode_by_name(   data,   method = "arcgis",   bbox = region_bbox("NJ"),   min_score = 90,   accept_types = c("PointAddress", "Subaddress", "StreetAddress"),   ... )

title: Name-based geocode pass (the
text: data data frame carrying record_id, record_name, city_clean, state_clean, geocode audit columns. method tidygeocoder method accepts free-text queries (default "arcgis"; "osm" "google" also work). bbox Bounding box used reject --region matches; see region_bbox(). min_score Minimum match score (0-100) name hit pass without review. Hits stay reviewable. Default 90. accept_types Address types precise enough pass without review (matched case-insensitively geocoder's addr_type). Default point-address types c("PointAddress", "Subaddress", "StreetAddress"). ... Passed tidygeocoder::geocode(). full_results = TRUE requested automatically scores available; pass full_results = FALSE opt (also disables score gating).

title: Name-based geocode pass (the
text: data name-lookup audit columns nm_latitude, nm_longitude, nm_score, nm_addr_type, nm_status, updated latitude/longitude/geocode_method/geocode_pass/match_status rows name pass filled. nothing tier geocode, nm_status set "not_run" audit clarity. Low-confidence fills also set review_status == "needs_manual_review".

title: Name-based geocode pass (the
text: name lookups looser address matching, hit scored using geocoder's match score address type (available - ArcGIS returns via full_results, pass requests automatically). hit passes cleanly resolves precise point address min_score; fuzzier hits (POI, locality centroid, low score) still coordinates filled context marked match_status == "matched_low_confidence" routed needs_manual_review person can confirm . geocoder returns score/type information (e.g. method = "osm"), pass falls back previous rule: -region match accepted. Filled rows tagged geocode_pass == "pass_4_name_lookup". bounding box still rejects --region hits, catch wrong -state match, exactly score gate .

what: Run the full geocoding cascade — geocode_records
title: Run the full geocoding cascade — geocode_records
text: Orchestrates tiered strategy already-cleaned, already-flagged frame (see clean_addresses() flag_bad_addresses()): Census structured match, ArcGIS address fallback, name lookup, validating configured region tier later tier retries still unplaced.

title: Run the full geocoding cascade — geocode_records
code: geocode_records(   data,   tiers = c("census", "arcgis", "name"),   reference = NULL,   boundary = NULL,   bbox = region_bbox("NJ"),   name_min_score = 90,   name_accept_types = c("PointAddress", "Subaddress", "StreetAddress"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run the full geocoding cascade — geocode_records
text: data data frame flag_bad_addresses(). tiers tiers run, order. subset c("census", "arcgis", "name"). reference Optional trusted key -> coordinates table Tier 0; see backfill_from_reference(). NULL skips Tier 0. non-default column names, call backfill_from_reference() geocode_records(). boundary Optional sf boundary polygon-precise validation; passed validate_geocodes(). NULL uses bounding box. bbox Bounding box region guards; see region_bbox(). name_min_score Minimum ArcGIS score name lookup pass without review. Passed geocode_by_name(). name_accept_types ArcGIS address types precise enough name lookup pass without review. Passed geocode_by_name(). verbose Whether print per-tier match tally.

title: Run the full geocoding cascade — geocode_records
text: data coordinates full audit trail populated.

title: Run the full geocoding cascade — geocode_records
text: tier records placed row geocode_pass, final frame self-documenting: pass_0_reference, pass_1_census_structured, pass_2_fallback, pass_4_name_lookup. cascade, valid matched rows marked review_status == "auto_accepted"; anything still unmatched lands needs_manual_review, invalid coordinates rejected. Supplying reference runs authoritative Tier 0 first (backfill_from_reference()): rows whose verified coordinates already known placed exactly skipped every later tier. Feed prior cycles' completed overrides back manual review accrues time.

what: locatr: Audit-Ready Geocoding and Local Geography for Messy Location Data — locatr-package
title: locatr: Audit-Ready Geocoding and Local Geography for Messy Location Data — locatr-package
text: Cleans, geocodes, validates, reviews, exports messy location address data supplied user. package sits top 'tidygeocoder': calls geocoding services, rejects implausible coordinates configurable region guards, applies fallback name/address matching, joins points optional local geography 'sf', records audit trail showing coordinate produced. Outputs designed manual review, dashboards, reusable location crosswalks.

title: locatr: Audit-Ready Geocoding and Local Geography for Messy Location Data — locatr-package
text: Maintainer: George Arthur prigasgenthian48@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: Region bounding box — region_bbox
text: Returns approximate latitude/longitude bounding box named region, used fast sanity check geocoded coordinates. Presets deliberately little generous legitimate edge locations rejected.

text: named numeric vector elements lat_min, lat_max, lon_min, lon_max.
